transliteration: asaktir anabhiṣvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛhādiṣu | nityaṃ ca sama-cittatvam iṣṭāniṣṭopapattiṣu ||
literal_meaning: Non-attachment / non-clinging / to son-wife-house-etc. / always / and / even-mindedness / in the gain of the desirable and undesirable.
contextual_meaning: True knowledge includes the capacity to hold intimate relationships without clinging, and to meet both welcome and unwelcome life-events with an even mind.
psychological_state: Secure attachment: close without fusion; open to loss without collapse.
trigger_scenario: A parent watching their child leave for college—tears in the eyes, warmth in the heart, no impulse to control.
anchor_text: Love deeply without clinging, and greet every gain or loss with steady calm.
